title: Historical Event
description: One major event from the historical record — a pandemic, war, famine, disaster, or collapse — from the dawn of civilization onward. Shares the claims/verification/provenance trust spine with the Threat and Event schemas; only the domain block and sort_keys differ. Chronology uses signed astronomical year numbering (0 = 1 BCE, -2999 = 3000 BCE) because ISO dates cannot represent BCE. This kind is deliberately exempt from staleness checks — an archive cannot go stale. Deliberately flat and free of pattern/min/max keywords so this exact schema can double as a Claude structured-output format; slug and range checks live in Python.

id.description: Stable slug, equals the filename without .json. Must match ^[a-z0-9-]+$ (enforced in Python).

year_start.description: Signed astronomical year the event began: 0 = 1 BCE, -2999 = 3000 BCE, 1918 = 1918 CE. Drives chronological ordering. Range checked in Python.

year_end.description: Signed astronomical year the event ended; null for a point event. Must be >= year_start (checked in Python).

date_display.description: Human-readable chronology, e.g. "c. 1177 BCE", "1347–1351", "79 CE". The frontend shows this, never the raw years.

era.description: Editorial era the frontend groups the timeline by. Guidance bands: ancient < 800 BCE; classical 800 BCE–499 CE; medieval 500–1449; early-modern 1450–1799; modern 1800–1944; contemporary 1945–.

country.description: Modern place name(s), e.g. "modern-day Iraq" — or a scope like "Eurasia" for continental events.

deaths_low.description: Lower bound of the estimated death toll as the cited source states it. Equal to deaths_high for a point estimate; null with deaths_high null when unquantified.

deaths_high.description: Upper bound of the estimated death toll. Must be >= deaths_low when both are set (checked in Python).

summary.description: States the estimate range and its uncertainty in prose, as the sources give it — never invented precision.

id.description: Stable per-event id, e.g. claim-1. Lets re-verification diff in place.

source_url.description: Empty string until grounded. Only the gate normalizes source_name from this.

retrieved_date.description: ISO-8601 date the source was last retrieved, or empty string if never.

chronology_rank.description: year_start + 10000 (the offset keeps ranks positive back to 9999 BCE); larger = later. Range checked in Python.

impact_rank.description: 1-5 from the midpoint of the deaths_low/deaths_high range; range enforced in Python.

composite.description: chronology_rank*10 + impact_rank. Chronology dominates; impact breaks same-year ties.
